comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

For `server_default` in SQLAlchemy models, it is generally recommended to use `sa.text("now()")` instead of `func.now()` for consistency with Alembic migrations and to explicitly indicate a database-side default. This aligns better with how the migration file defines the default.

```suggestion
        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.text("now()")
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The introduction of database persistence for slot leases significantly impacts persisted payloads and in-flight workflows for crash recovery. As per the repository's style guide, please consider adding compatibility or replay-style regression tests, or document the cutover process to ensure in-flight safety during manager restarts.

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. If a change can affect already-running workflows or persisted payloads, add a compatibility or replay-style regression test, or document why in-flight compatibility is impossible and how the cutover is made safe. <sup>([link](https://github.com/MoonLadderStudios/MoonMind/blob/main/.gemini/styleguide.md))</sup>
</details>

## Code Review

This pull request implements database persistence for AuthProfileManager slot leases to enable crash recovery. It introduces the AuthProfileSlotLease model, a corresponding migration, and a new Temporal activity to handle database operations. The AuthProfileManager workflow is updated to synchronize lease state during slot assignment and release, and to restore state upon startup. Feedback includes a suggestion to use `sa.text("now()")` for database-side defaults in the SQLAlchemy model and a requirement to add compatibility or replay tests to ensure safety for in-flight workflows as per the repository style guide.

Persisting leases via `_sync_leases_to_db()` adds a new activity to the workflow history. This must be gated behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` id to avoid nondeterminism for existing histories. Also note that leases are modified outside `_drain_queue` (e.g. `_verify_lease_holders` and `_evict_expired_leases` release leases), so only syncing here can leave the DB state stale for crash recovery.
```suggestion
        # Persist lease changes to DB for crash recovery.
        # Guard this behind a replay-stable patch ID to avoid nondeterminism
        # for existing workflow histories.
        if leases_changed and workflow.patched("auth-profile-manager-sync-leases-v1"):
```

When persisting leases, the payload currently omits the in-memory `lease_granted_at` timestamps. The DB activity then sets `granted_at` to `now()`, so after a crash restore the lease age will be wrong and max-lease eviction can be delayed. Consider including the existing ISO timestamp per workflow_id in the persisted data and preserving it on upsert.
```suggestion
                    lease = {
                        "workflow_id": wf_id,
                        "profile_id": profile.profile_id,
                    }
                    # Preserve original lease grant time if available so that
                    # restored leases have correct age for eviction decisions.
                    if getattr(profile, "lease_granted_at", None):
                        granted_at = profile.lease_granted_at.get(wf_id)
                        if granted_at is not None:
                            lease["granted_at"] = granted_at
                    leases.append(lease)
```

In the `save` path this implementation only deletes rows for workflow_ids that are being re-inserted. It never removes rows that exist in DB but are no longer present in `leases` (and if `leases` is empty it returns without clearing anything), so stale leases will accumulate and be incorrectly “restored” after a crash. The save operation should either replace the full set (e.g., delete all rows for `runtime_id` then insert current leases) or explicitly delete rows not in the incoming set.
```suggestion
                # Replace the full lease set for this runtime_id to avoid stale leases.
                await session.execute(
                    delete(AuthProfileSlotLease).where(
                        AuthProfileSlotLease.runtime_id == runtime_id,
                    )
                )
                saved_count = 0
                for lease in leases or []:
                    workflow_id = lease.get("workflow_id")
                    profile_id = lease.get("profile_id")
                    if not workflow_id or not profile_id:
                        continue
```

`granted_at` is always overwritten with `datetime.now()` on every save (because the row is deleted then re-inserted). That means the persisted lease “age” no longer represents when the lease was granted, and after crash recovery the manager may keep leases longer than intended. Prefer preserving the original `granted_at` (either by upserting without delete, or by accepting/persisting the workflow-provided timestamp).

New crash-recovery behavior (DB sync + restore) isn’t covered by the existing AuthProfileManager unit tests in `tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_auth_profile_manager.py`. Adding tests that assert (a) lease changes trigger the expected activity calls (behind a patch gate) and (b) restored leases populate `current_leases`/`lease_granted_at` and reconnect signals would help prevent regressions.
```suggestion
        # Persist lease changes to DB for crash recovery (behind a patch gate for determinism)
        if leases_changed and workflow.patched("auth_profile_manager_db_sync_v1"):
```

`release_slot` now schedules an activity (`_remove_lease_from_db`). That changes recorded workflow history when this signal is delivered and will cause nondeterminism when replaying existing executions unless it’s gated behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` id (per docs/Temporal/WorkerDeployment.md). Also, the DB lease should be removed even when `profile_id` is unknown (e.g. profile deleted/disabled) so we don’t leave stale rows behind.
```suggestion

        # Gate new activity behavior behind a replay-stable patch ID.
        # Old histories (pre-patch) will only release the in-memory profile
        # state; new histories will also remove the DB lease, even if the
        # profile is no longer present in _profiles.
        if workflow.patched("auth_profile_manager_release_slot_remove_lease"):
            if profile:
                profile.release(requester_id)
            await self._remove_lease_from_db(requester_id)
        else:
            if profile:
                profile.release(requester_id)
```

This startup call to `_load_leases_from_db()` introduces a new activity command during `run()`. For in-flight AuthProfileManager executions created before this change, replay will now try to schedule an activity that doesn’t exist in their history, causing nondeterminism. Please gate this new branch behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched("...")` id (see docs/Temporal/WorkerDeployment.md).
```suggestion
        if (
            workflow.patched("AuthProfileManager.load_leases_on_startup")
            and not has_leases
            and not has_pending
        ):
```

## Pull request overview This PR improves AuthProfileManager slot handling by persisting active auth-profile “slot leases” to Postgres so the manager can restore leases and reconnect to running workflows after a crash/restart. **Changes:** - Persist in-memory lease state to the DB and load it on startup to avoid orphaned slot holders. - Add a new Temporal activity (`auth_profile.sync_slot_leases`) and register it in the activity runtime/catalog. - Introduce a new DB table/model + migration (`auth_profile_slot_leases`) to store runtime/workflow/profile lease rows. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/auth_profile_manager.py | Adds DB-backed lease persistence, restore-on-startup logic, and removes leases from DB on release. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/artifacts.py | Implements the `auth_profile.sync_slot_leases` activity (load/save/remove) using SQLAlchemy. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py | Registers the new activity type -> handler binding. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py | Adds the new activity definition to the default catalog. | | api_service/migrations/versions/c3d4e5f6a7b8_add_auth_profile_slot_leases_table.py | Creates the `auth_profile_slot_leases` table + indexes/unique constraint. | | api_service/db/models.py | Adds the `AuthProfileSlotLease` ORM model. |
